signals.reasoning: BTC remains structurally firm, but the active veto means there is no justification to chase a fresh long and no clean short edge either.
ETH has mild trend-supported strength, but under veto it stays watch-only rather than a deployable long.
SOL still reads more like a short-covering bounce than a clean directional trade, so I would not force either side here.
STBL remains one of the better bearish fades because crowding is still elevated and predicted funding points back toward expensive long positioning.
FARTCOIN is still a crowded-upside fade with very high open-interest expansion, positive funding, and an already extended move.
The symbol is too illiquid and too wide for a serious new trade, especially in a veto regime.
XPL is moving up, but there is not enough crowding to make it a strong short and veto blocks treating it as a chase long.
AVNT still looks like a short-covering rally rather than durable strength, so it remains a reasonable secondary fade.
WLD has upside momentum but not enough confirmation or crowding to override the event brake.
FOGO already looks flushed and is no longer an attractive fresh short entry.
TON is strong but extended, and under veto that makes it watch-only rather than a chase long.
